what_it_does: This file enumerates verifiable completion criteria for the 'self-serve core' build scope (scope C), organized into search hierarchy, onboarding, gap closures, MCP coherence, and frontend exposure, and states the verification rules the build loop must use to check each item.
observations: Every criterion must be verified by real use on real data, never by reading code.
Criteria are split into FUNCTION and FORM bars; FORM is declared to be half of done and requires a design-critic pass plus design-lint.
A strict reuse-don't-parallel gate requires each item to extend an existing primitive; introducing a parallel engine/registry/parser fails review.
Status is honest: ☐ means not verified, ☑ means verified by use; no code-reading greens.
C1 defines a UNION between live-edge session_recall and deep-corpus recollection at a freshness seam.
C2 composes existing primitives (capabilities, start_guide, supervisor mailbox, guide board-items) into a single onboarding entry point.
C3 covers self-serve era clones, clone discoverability in common channels, channel rules/project-info, and new attachment types.
C4 requires MCP coherence: scoped search, discovery, health checks, and a single bootstrap door.
C5 frontend exposure is FORM-heavy and is explicitly last in priority.
A Product Face group adds cross-cutting design-system criteria for all scope-C surfaces.
Priority order is C1, C2, C3, C4, C5.
The file cross-references IMPLEMENTATION_GUIDE.md and RESEARCH_SYNTHESIS.md.
standouts: The hard 'verified by use' rule and explicit ban on code-reading verification.
The live-edge ⊕ deep-corpus UNION architecture at a freshness seam.
The reuse-don't-parallel gate as a hard review failure condition.
FORM being weighted as half of done, requiring both a design-critic pass and design-lint.
conventions: Markdown heading hierarchy with numbered criteria.
Each criterion has a one-line FUNCTION statement followed by a ☐ verified by use checkbox.
Frontend criteria pair a FUNCTION bar with a FORM bar.
Cross-references to sibling build-prep files at the end.
Scope naming using C1, C2, C3, C4, C5 and PF prefixes.
concerns: The file asserts all items are ☐ today, but as a static doc it can become stale without a living status tracker.
It depends heavily on sibling files (RESEARCH_SYNTHESIS.md, IMPLEMENTATION_GUIDE.md) and is not self-contained.
'Verified by use' leaves undefined what counts as a 'real session', 'real query', or 'real spawn'.
The reuse-don't-parallel gate, if read rigidly, could block legitimate refactorings that temporarily overlap primitives.
notes: This is the scope-C truth-table: what the self-serve core must demonstrably do, with strict by-use verification and design-system form gates.
questions: What exactly constitutes a 'real session', 'real query', or 'real spawn' for verification?
Where are verification results recorded when a checkbox is marked ☑?
How is the freshness-seam deduplication implemented and measured?
What is the specific relationship between mcp_face and company-channel bootstrap that C4.4 wants resolved?
purpose_vs_actual: Matches: it presents itself as a build-loop truth-table and does so by listing verifiable by-use criteria, verification rules, priority order, and cross-references.
apparent_themes: Verification by real use, not code inspection.
Design-system fidelity as a first-class completion bar.
Reuse and extension of existing primitives over parallel invention.
Coherent MCP exposure of backend capabilities.
intention_signals: It is trying to prevent 'green-painted' completion by forcing every claimed feature to be exercised on real data and every frontend surface to pass a design rubric, while keeping the build from sprouting duplicate engines or registries.
novelty: The explicit FUNCTION/FORM bar split with FORM weighted as half of done, and the reuse-don't-parallel gate as a hard review failure, are unusual for a completion-criteria document.
contribution: A rigorous by-use verification framework and a prioritized checklist that ties backend capabilities, MCP exposure, and design-system frontend surfaces into one completion rubric.
summary_for_embedding: company/build-prep/self-serve-core/COMPLETION_CRITERIA.md defines the verifiable completion criteria for the 'self-serve core' scope, covering search hierarchy, onboarding, gap closures, MCP coherence, and frontend exposure, with strict by-use verification and design-system form gates.
intention_for_embedding: To serve as the build loop's truth-table for scope C, ensuring every deliverable is validated by real use and conforms to the design system before being marked complete.
